lede: A look at July 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Álex Baena and Gisele Bündchen, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Jeff Bezos
events.desc: American businessman Jeff Bezos flies to space aboard New Shepard NS-16 operated by his private spaceflight company Blue Origin.

events.title: Suruç bombing
events.desc: A huge explosion in the mostly Kurdish border town of Suruç, Turkey, targeting the Socialist Youth Associations Federation, kills at least 31 people and injures over 100.

events.title: James Holmes (mass murderer)
events.desc: James Holmes opened fire at a movie theater in Aurora, Colorado, killing 12 and injuring 70 others.

events.title: USS Constitution
events.desc: The fully restored USS Constitution (a.k.a. Old Ironsides) celebrates its 200th birthday by setting sail for the first time in 116 years.

events.title: Johnstown flood of 1977
events.desc: The Johnstown flood of 1977 kills 84 people and causes millions of dollars in damages.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight B-2
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight B-2 crashes after takeoff from Vitim Airport in the Sakha Republic, killing 39.

events.title: Viking 1
events.desc: The American Viking 1 lander successfully lands on Mars.

events.title: Abdullah I of Jordan
events.desc: King Abdullah I of Jordan is assassinated by a Palestinian while attending Friday prayers in Jerusalem.

events.title: California
events.desc: California opens its first freeway, the Arroyo Seco Parkway.

events.title: Seneca Falls Convention
events.desc: The first Women's Rights Convention in Seneca Falls, New York, a two-day event, concludes.

deaths.text: Abdullah I of Jordan, king of Jordan
Anna Gardie, French-American dancer and actress (born c. 1760)

observances.text: Christian feast day: Elizabeth Cady Stanton, Amelia Bloomer, Sojourner Truth, and Harriet Ross Tubman (Episcopal Church (USA))
